destinations.evidence: The port now defines audio_escrow_lockup using max(audio_min_fee_wei, audio_fee_wei_per_second * AUDIO_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS) and extracts the multimodal model name from ABI slot 14.
destinations.snippet: pub fn audio_escrow_lockup(...) -> Result<U256, String> { ... Ok(duration_fee.max(min_fee)) }

destinations.evidence: ModelRegistry reads now populate audio_fee_wei_per_second and audio_min_fee_wei from slots 7 and 8, matching current downstream model-specific multimodal pricing.
destinations.snippet: base_slot + U256::from(7) ... audioFeeWeiPerSecond; base_slot + U256::from(8) ... audioMinFeeWei

destinations.evidence: Wallet validation now supports AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE, uses audio_escrow_lockup for executor escrow, and includes the long-running delivery callback cost for audio inputs.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port after correcting the stale tier wording to the current downstream model-specific rule: audio wallet validation now requires worst-case audio executor escrow based on model registry pricing and max supported duration, not input size.

related_but_insufficient_code: wallet.rs handles LLM, HTTP, DKMS, ZK, SOVEREIGN_AGENT, and PERSISTENT_AGENT escrow paths but has no AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE branch.
ritual-precompile-addresses registers AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE, but registration does not establish wallet escrow pricing.

notes: Mapped after local parity fix. The catalog wording was also corrected because current downstream no longer uses an AUDIO_TIER3_FEE_WEI constant; it uses model-specific audio pricing at AUDIO_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS.
rationale: The original tier-3 wording was superseded by model-specific multimodal pricing: current downstream reads audio pricing from the model registry and defines audio_escrow_lockup as the maximum of the configured minimum and per-second fee multiplied by AUDIO_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS. The durable behavior is that audio wallet validation reserves worst-case executor escrow for the workload instead of using input-size HTTP-style pricing.
statement: Wallet validation for audio async calls must require model-specific worst-case audio escrow for the maximum supported audio duration, rather than estimating executor escrow from input size.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run wallet validation for a 0x0819 audio async transaction backed by model registry audio pricing and assert the required executor escrow is max(audio_min_fee_wei, audio_fee_wei_per_second * AUDIO_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS) regardless of input byte length; verify insufficient balance below that escrow is rejected.
summary: Audio wallet escrow pricing is mapped in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Local ritual-async-fees unit tests cover audio wallet validation below and at the required model-specific worst-case escrow plus commitment, inclusion, and callback costs.
title: Audio escrow covers worst-case duration
